Agastache is a genus of flowering plants in the mint family (Lamiaceae), commonly known as giant hyssop or agastache. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Agastache include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Agastache is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
